fieldHelp.perProviderRetry: Extra retries allowed on the same channel after it fails; after this limit, failover mode switches to the next channel and single-channel proxy returns an error.
fieldHelp.maxRetry: Total extra retries allowed across all channels for one request, limiting overall latency.
fieldHelp.retryInterval: Time to wait before retrying the same channel; set to 0 to retry immediately.
fieldHelp.retryableStatusCodes: Which upstream HTTP status codes may trigger same-channel retry or cross-channel failover. Supports comma-separated values and ranges such as 400,429,500-599; only 400-599 are allowed. Connection failures, timeouts, and empty responses are not controlled by this list.
fieldHelp.firstByteTimeout: Maximum time to wait for the first upstream chunk after a streaming HTTP 200 response.
fieldHelp.idleTimeout: Maximum idle time allowed between chunks during a streaming response.
fieldHelp.nonStreamingTimeout: Maximum time to wait for a complete non-streaming upstream response.
fieldHelp.failureThreshold: When one provider-model accumulates this failure score within the failure window, it enters the cooldown pool.
fieldHelp.failureWindow: Only failures inside this recent time window are counted; older failures expire gradually.
fieldHelp.baseCooldown: Minimum cooldown after the first circuit break. The model enters probing after the cooldown.
fieldHelp.maxCooldown: Upper bound for cooldown after repeated circuit breaks, so backoff cannot grow without limit.
fieldHelp.probeSuccess: Number of consecutive successful probes required before the model is treated as healthy again.

hints.wslHost: Address for WSL-side CLI to reach the gateway instead of 127.0.0.1; leave empty to skip
hints.thinkingRectifier: When enabled, Claude inbound requests that hit thinking/signature compatibility 4xx errors will remove thinking blocks, signature fields, and the top-level thinking parameter, then retry once on the same channel. Normal protocol conversion preserves output_config.effort to reasoning effort mapping.
hints.thinkingBudgetRectifier: When the target Anthropic upstream returns a budget_tokens-related 4xx, adjust the budget and retry once on the same channel.
hints.cacheInjection: Inject cache_control markers into target Anthropic requests to reduce repeated context cost.
hints.lossyRejection: Off by default: detected lossy protocol conversions continue best-effort and add X-Transformer-Lossy to the response headers. When enabled, clearly irreversible conversions return a local 400 and do not affect provider health or failover.
hints.responsesEncryptedContentRectifier: For OpenAI Responses targets, when the upstream explicitly reports encrypted_content verification or decryption failure, remove only the invalid encrypted reasoning items and retry once on the same channel.

__main_agents_separator__.name: Main Agents
__main_agents_separator__.description: Main Agents: Direct entry points for users, responsible for coordination and decision-making (you actively seek them)

sisyphus.name: Sisyphus
sisyphus.description: Primary orchestrator - Your "first receptionist", receives requests and decides to handle or delegate, responsible for task classification, delegation and global coordination

hephaestus.name: Hephaestus
hephaestus.description: Autonomous deep worker - A "senior craftsman" who just needs goals, spends 5-15 minutes researching before acting, completes complex tasks end-to-end without step-by-step guidance

prometheus.name: Prometheus
prometheus.description: Strategic planner - A "product manager" who only plans without coding, breaks big tasks into small steps, generates dependency graphs and execution plans

atlas.name: Atlas
atlas.description: Task manager - A "project manager" with todo list, tracks multi-step task progress, ensures each step is completed in order without omission (automatically invoked by system)

__sub_agents_separator__.name: Sub Agents
__sub_agents_separator__.description: Sub Agents: Domain experts, invoked by main agents or system (they work passively)

oracle.name: Oracle
oracle.description: Strategic advisor - A "CTO consultant" who only observes, specializes in architecture analysis, code review, debugging, gives advice but does not modify code

librarian.name: Librarian
librarian.description: Multi-repo researcher - A "librarian" for external resources, searches GitHub code, reads official docs, finds open source implementation examples

explore.name: Explore
explore.description: Fast codebase search - Project-level "Ctrl+Shift+F", quickly locates "where is this feature", "who calls this function", can run multiple in parallel

multimodal-looker.name: Multimodal-Looker
multimodal-looker.description: Media analyzer - An assistant with "eyes", specializes in viewing images, PDFs, design drafts, extracts visual information into text descriptions

metis.name: Metis
metis.description: Pre-planning analyst - Prometheus's "pre-analyst", helps clarify "what you really want" before planning, discovers hidden requirements and potential pitfalls

momus.name: Momus
momus.description: Plan reviewer - Prometheus's "QA inspector", identifies blocking issues in plans, ensures plans are executable, references are valid, nothing is missed

sisyphus-junior.name: Sisyphus-Junior

inventory.agentPromptText:
Please use your file read/write tools to reorganize this AI Toolbox Skills Inventory JSON file: {{path}}

Goal: reorganize Skill groups, notes, ordering, and enabled states, then write the result to an importable JSON file.

Constraints:
1. Prefer reading {{path}} with tools. Do not ask me to paste a large JSON blob manually.
2. Save the final result as a JSON file and tell me the output path; do not only print a huge JSON block in chat.
3. The JSON content must be directly importable by AI Toolbox. Do not wrap it in Markdown fences and do not mix in explanatory text.
4. Preserve the top-level schema_version field.
5. Do not add, delete, or modify core records. Preserve every skill's id, source_ref, central_path, and content_hash exactly.
6. Do not output a description field.
7. Any local skill omitted from the JSON will be disabled by default, so do not drop any skill entries.
8. You may modify the groups array (add, rename, reorder), but group names must remain unique.
9. skill.group must reference an existing groups.name or be null.
10. You may modify each skill's user_note, order, enabled, and previous_enabled_tools fields. Disabled skills (enabled: false) may still remain categorized in groups.
